items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: Chuah, Lukut and Bagan Pinang contests take shape
items.summary: The Election Commission (EC) confirmed today that Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Datuk Yew Boon Lye will defend Chuah against Pau Jeou Ching of Barisan Nasional (BN) after nominations closed. The first was from the PH candidate at 9.02am, followed by the BN candidate at 9.06am. In Sri Tanjung, incumbent Datuk Dr G Rajasekaran of PH will face BN's A. Achutan, M. Leevineshwaraan of Bersatu, Datuk A. Saravanan, an independent candidate, and Islah Wahyudi Zainudin, also an independent candidate.

items.title: Man, 66, arrested over rape of disabled teen in Tawau
items.summary: TAWAU: A 66-year-old man has been arrested and remanded for seven days over the rape of a 15-year-old girl with disabilities. Acting Tawau police chief Superintendent Wilynton Enchana Watt said the case came to light after the teenager told her adoptive parent that she was experiencing bodily pain. Wilynton said the suspect is being investigated under Section 376(1) of the Penal Code for rape.

items.title: Don’t turn adat into campaign fodder, Mohamad Hasan warns Negeri Sembilan BN party machinery
items.summary: REMBAU, July 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has reminded the party’s election machinery not to use Negeri Sembilan’s adat (traditional customs) as a campaign issue in the 16th state election, saying the institution must be protected from political disputes. Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, said the state’s adat institution should be respected and kept separate from politics to prevent unnecessary tensions during the democratic process. “I have made it clear and reminded everyone that during the election campaign, we must not touch on adat issues.

items.title: Lipis route under police watch amid migrant smuggling attempts
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Lipis stretch remains under close police surveillance as smuggling syndicates continue to use the route to ferry undocumented migrants from a neighbouring country into Malaysia. Last year, police arrested 56 undocumented Myanmar nationals – 29 men and 27 women – along with eight Malaysians who acted as transporters for the migrants during a series of integrated operations in Lipis. Pahang acting police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the undocumented migrants were believed to have been smuggled through Kelantan before entering Pahang.

items.title: Fahmi says Pakatan will let Tok Min’s track record do the talking as race to keep Negeri Sembilan begins
items.summary: JEMPOL, July 18 — The Pakatan Harapan (PH) campaign for the Negeri Sembilan state election will centre on maintaining administrative continuity and highlighting Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun’s track record in leading the state since 2018. PH Communications director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said the coalition would focus on Aminuddin’s leadership record and the achievements of the state administration, which he said had delivered positive economic outcomes for Negeri Sembilan. Fahmi said he would also monitor the welfare of media practitioners throughout the state election campaign.

items.title: 4 killed, 4 missing in Vietnam flash floods
items.summary: HANOI: At least four people were killed and four others were missing after a flash flood swept through a mountainous village in Vietnam's northern province of Lai Chau, state media reported on Saturday. The flood in Muong Than village early on Friday after days of heavy rains in several parts of northern Vietnam also injured seven people, reported the Vietnam News Agency. Natural disasters, mostly floods, killed 489 people in the country last year, according to government data.

items.title: Nilai turns five-way as DAP’s Arul Kumar faces crowded field
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 18 — Nilai assemblyman and DAP national vice-chairman J. Arul Kumar faces a tough challenge in a five-cornered contest in the upcoming 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. The Nilai seat has drawn a crowded field, with incumbent Arul Kumar of Pakatan Harapan (PH) up against Zamani Ibrahim (Berjasa), Datuk Lai Chien Kong (Barisan Nasional), Datuk V. Saravana Kumar (Bersatu) and independent candidate Omar Mohd Isa. Over in the Lenggeng seat, PH candidate Zarinna Abu Zarin will take on incumbent Datuk Mohd Asna Amin from BN, alongside Bersatu’s Zool Amali Hussin.

items.title: PH banking on Aminuddin's MB track record in Negri polls, says Fahmi
items.summary: PH communications director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said Aminuddin's administration had delivered tangible improvements in the economy, development and zakat collection. This will be one of our key campaign messages throughout the state election," he said. Asked about cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional, Fahmi said it would not hinder PH's efforts to retain the state.

items.title: A ‘50-50 seat’: Anthony Loke banks on Pakatan’s track record as he fights to retain mixed-majority Chennah
items.summary: JELEBU, July 18 — The Chennah state seat is among the four most crucial constituencies in the Jelebu parliamentary constituency for Pakatan Harapan (PH) as it seeks to retain the Negeri Sembilan government in the upcoming state election, incumbent Anthony Loke said. Earlier, Jelebu Parliamentary returning officer Abdul Rahim A Aziz announced that the Chennah state seat would witness a straight fight between Loke and Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Siow Kong Choon. Commenting on his chances of retaining Chennah, a mixed-majority seat, following the recent Johor state election in which DAP lost several semi-urban constituencies, Loke said every election has its own dynamics.

items.title: Police warn parties against touching sensitive issues during Negri Sembilan campaign
items.summary: Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said candidates and party supporters must refrain from making provocative, inflammatory or defamatory statements that could spark public tension, hatred or racial division. Alzafny also warned that enforcement action would be taken against candidates or supporters who breached traffic and campaign regulations, including riding motorcycles without helmets during election-related activities. Meanwhile, Alzafny said the nomination process for the Negri Sembilan election proceeded smoothly, with all eight nomination centres completing the process without any major issues.

items.title: BN unfazed by three-cornered fight in Juasseh
items.summary: KUALA PILAH: Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate for Juasseh Datuk Ismail Lasim is unfazed by the three-cornered contest for the seat in the Negri Sembilan election. Ismail said he respected the decision of all candidates to offer themselves to the voters and was ready to face the contest. Ismail will face Pakatan Harapan's Mohd Aidil Abdullah and Bersatu's Mohd Zuhaimi Md Yusof.

items.title: Onn Hafiz kicks off second term with ‘no honeymoon’ warning for new Johor exco
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 18 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has reminded the state’s newly appointed executive councillors (Exco) that there will be no “honeymoon” period, urging them instead to get straight to work in fulfilling their responsibilities to the people. Onn Hafiz said the new term would see continuity in the state administration, with six incumbent executive councillors retained and four new faces appointed to strengthen the state government machinery. The six retained executive councillors are Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor, Mohd Hairi Mad Shah, Mohd Fared Mohd Khalid, Ling Tian Soon, Lee Ting Han and Mohamad Fazli Mohamad Salleh.

items.title: Man, 72, arrested for drink-driving after Manjung crash
items.summary: MANJUNG: A 72-year-old man was arrested last night for driving under the influence of alcohol following a two-vehicle accident at Jalan Lumut-Sitiawan here. The 11.20pm incident involved a Mercedes-Benz driven by the man and a Honda driven by a woman. "The man was later released on police bail pending completion of investigations.

items.title: Zahid says BN-Perikatan tie-up is ‘understanding’, not pact, as coalition eyes Negeri Sembilan comeback
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today insisted his coalition’s electoral cooperation with Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Negeri Sembilan state election was an “understanding”, not a formal political pact. Speaking after accompanying BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, who is defending the Rantau state seat, at the nomination centre in Rembau, Zahid said the tactical seat-sharing arrangement would help BN regain control of Negeri Sembilan. “God willing, with this tactical approach, Negeri Sembilan will once again be governed by BN.

items.title: QR code checks and tighter controls: Govt moves to tighten cooking oil subsidy scheme
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 18 — The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) will scrutinise the Public Accounts Committee’s (PAC) recommendations on cooking oil price control and subsidy management, including expediting the Cooking Oil Stabilisation Scheme System (eCOSS) rollout to facilitate targeted subsidies and address subsidy leakages. He said KPDN welcomed the PAC’s recommendations on the management of cooking oil price controls and subsidies under the ministry, as presented in PAC Report DR. 27 of 2026 in the Dewan Rakyat on July 16. “Since the Cooking Oil Stabilisation Scheme (COSS) was introduced, the government has not determined quotas for refineries.

items.title: Jelebu’s three state seats head for three-way contests, former schoolmates among rivals
items.summary: JELEBU, July 18 — There will be three-cornered fights for the Pertang, Klawang and Sungai Lui state seats in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election. For the Pertang state seat, incumbent Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias of Barisan Nasional (BN) will be challenged by Mohd Umry Abdul Khois of Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Mohd Faizal Fadli Mohd Idrus of Bersatu. The Klawang state seat will also witness a three-cornered fight involving incumbent Datuk Bakri Sawir (PH), Muhammad Adib Musa (Bersatu) and Danni Rais (PN).

items.title: Onn Hafiz to new Johor exco: No honeymoon, time to deliver
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Menteri Besar Onn Hafiz Ghazi has told his new state executive council (Exco) lineup that there will be no honeymoon period following their appointment today. He said everyone must get to work immediately to deliver on the mandate given by the people and realise the Maju Johor 2030 agenda. He said the state administration's priority is to implement the Maju Johor 2030 agenda to build a more developed and prosperous Johor.

items.title: One seat, four candidates: Jeram Padang stands out in Negeri Sembilan polls as only four-way clash
items.summary: JEMPOL, July 18 — Jeram Padang is the only seat in the Jempol state constituency to witness a four-cornered contest in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election, with Serting and Palong seeing three-cornered contests and Bahau set for a straight fight. The Jeram Padang state seat will see Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate G. Manivannan go up against incumbent Datuk Mohd Zaidy Abdul Kadir of Barisan Nasional (BN), R. Sri Sanjeevan of Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) and Dayana Dal of Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (ASLI), the only Orang Asli candidate vying for the seat. Meanwhile, the Serting state seat is set for a three-cornered battle between PH candidate Yaacob Mahmood, incumbent Mohd Fairuz Mohd Isa of Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Muhammad Noraffendy Mohd Salleh @ Affendy Salleh of Bersatu.

items.title: Zahid: BN-PN cooperation based on understanding, not formal pact
items.summary: REMBAU: The cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Negri Sembilan election is an understanding, and not an agreement or pact, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. The BN chairman said the coalition believed that through the tactical move, with BN contesting 25 seats and PN 11 seats, BN would be able to regain control of Negri Sembilan. "This understanding is important for us because previously BN only won 14 seats.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: Aminuddin admits tough battle ahead in BN stronghold Linggi
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said his chances of winning the Linggi seat in the state election remained "50-50". The Negri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, however, expressed confidence that voters would support the coalition's efforts to ensure a stable government. I left Sikamat because PH needs to win new seats to ensure a stable government," he said.

items.title: Johor’s new exco line-up sworn in as Onn Hafiz begins second term
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 18 — Ten Johor assemblymen took their oaths of office today as members of the Johor State Executive Council (Exco) before the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, at Istana Bukit Serene. The new Exco line-up includes Mohd Hairi Mad Shah (Larkin), Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor (Bukit Permai), Mohd Fared Mohd Khalid (Semerah), Ling Tian Soon (Yong Peng), Lee Ting Han (Paloh) and Mohamad Fazli Mohamad Salleh (Bukit Pasir). Present at the ceremony were Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, Royal Court Council Yang Dipertua Datuk Dr Abdul Rahim Ramli and State Secretary Datuk Mohammed Ridha Abd Kadir.

items.title: PN confident understanding with BN can deliver Negri Sembilan victory
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Perikatan Nasional (PN) is confident that its understanding with Barisan Nasional (BN), which has created a 'blue wave', can capture Negri Sembilan in the state election. "Our feeling as a group within PN heading into the Negri Sembilan election is different compared with Johor. Hamzah said the coalition also wanted BN's victory in Johor to be repeated in Negri Sembilan.

items.title: Rantau showdown set as Umno veteran Tok Mat takes on young doctor from Pakatan
items.summary: REMBAU, July 18 — The Rantau state seat is set for a straight fight in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, with state Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman and Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan facing Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Dr Azizul Hakim Mahdi. Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, has held the Rantau state seat since 2004. In the Chembong state seat, incumbent and BN candidate Datuk Zaifulbahri Idris will face a straight fight against PH candidate Danish Nazran Murad.

items.title: 103 candidates confirmed for 36-seat Negri Sembilan election
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A total of 103 candidates from various political parties and independent candidates will contest the 36 seats up for grabs in the Negri Sembilan election. He said of the total, Pakatan Harapan (PH) is fielding 36 candidates, followed by Barisan Nasional (BN) with 25 candidates, Bersatu with 24 candidates and Perikatan Nasional (PN) with 11 candidates. He said based on age categories, three candidates are aged between 18 and 29, 12 candidates are aged between 30 and 39, 39 candidates are aged between 40 and 49, 33 candidates are aged between 50 and 59, while 16 candidates are aged 60 and above.

items.title: Johor exco line-up unveiled with four new faces
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Four new faces have been appointed to the Johor state executive council (Exco), while six existing members have been retained. The new members are Md Israk Abdullah (Kukup), Dr Mohd Naqib Ghazali (Panti), Hasrunizah Hassan (Pulai Sebatang), and P. Pannir Selvam (Perling). Md Israk takes over the Agriculture, Agro-based Industry and Rural Development portfolio, while Dr Mohd Naqib handles Education and Information.

items.title: Pakatan’s Aminuddin swaps safe seat for Linggi gamble, gives himself 50-50 chance to break Umno grip
items.summary: By Muhammad Yusry PORT DICKSON, July 18 — Pakatan Harapan’s Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun today acknowledged that his bid to capture Linggi from Barisan Nasional (BN) will be an uphill battle, describing the contest against one of Umno’s longest-held strongholds in the state as a “50-50” race. The PKR vice-president who is also caretaker menteri besar said he is not taking the challenge lightly as he attempts to unseat incumbent Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli in Linggi, which has never elected a non-Umno assemblyman since it was first contested in 1959. Aminuddin will face a three-cornered fight against Mohd Faizal and Perikatan Nasional’s Datuk Zamri Md Said.

items.title: Earthquakes on the rise? Experts say no, but preparedness remains key
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Reports of earthquakes have become an almost daily occurrence, with powerful tremors striking different parts of the world and raising concerns over whether global seismic activity is increasing. In recent months, strong earthquakes have also hit countries along the Pacific Ring of Fire, including the Philippines, Indonesia, Japan, Chile and Tonga, prompting questions about whether Malaysia, which lies close to one of the world's most active seismic zones, is at greater risk. Pacific Ring of Fire remains the world's most active seismic zone Nearly 90 per cent of the world's earthquakes occur along the 40,000km Pacific Ring of Fire, which stretches from New Zealand through Papua New Guinea, Indonesia, the Philippines, Japan, Russia and Alaska before reaching the western coasts of North and South America.

items.title: Johari: BN-PN cooperation depends on common ground, not ideology
items.summary: TAMPIN: Any cooperation or understanding between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) will depend on both sides' willingness to find common ground despite their differing political ideologies. Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said ideological differences were among the main factors contributing to the different positions taken by political parties. However, he said if there was room to reach an understanding in certain areas or states, the matter would depend on the consideration of the respective parties.

items.title: Jalaluddin faces three-way Pertang fight, Loke in Chennah duel
items.summary: For the Pertang seat, Jalaluddin, who is also state Umno liaison committee chairman, will face a three-cornered contest against Pakatan Harapan's (PH) Mohd Umry Abdul Khois and Bersatu's Mohd Faizal Fadli Mohd Idrus. For the Klawang seat, PH's Datuk Bakri Sawir will defend his seat in a three-cornered contest against his cousin Danni Rais, who is representing Perikatan Nasional (PN), and Bersatu candidate Muhammad Adib Musa. In the Sungai Lui seat, BN candidate Datuk Mohd Razi Mohd Ali will seek to defend the constituency he has held for three terms, facing challenges from PH's Zainal Fikri Abd Kadir and Bersatu's Mazrulhisham Abd Mansor.

items.title: Negri polls will test claims DAP is losing support, says Loke
items.summary: JELEBU: Claims of declining support for DAP will ultimately be determined by the outcome of the Negri Sembilan election, says party secretary-general Anthony Loke. Separately, Loke said the state seats within the Jelebu parliamentary constituency are crucial to Pakatan Harapan's efforts to form the next state government. "In 2018, we succeeded in forming the state government because of the contribution from the two seats in the Jelebu parliamentary constituency, namely Chennah and Klawang.

items.title: Health Ministry gets 66,000 new posts boost to strengthen public healthcare
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 18 — The Public Service Department (JPA) has approved the creation of 66,312 new non-trade-off posts for the Ministry of Health (MOH), involving a financial implication of RM6.7 billion to strengthen the nation’s public healthcare system. In a statement today, the JPA said it had approved the creation of 53,516 new non-trade-off posts to support the operation of new healthcare facilities, upgrade projects for existing facilities and enhance the delivery of healthcare services to the public, with a financial implication of RM4.5 billion. The JPA, together with the Ministry of Finance, is also reviewing an application to create 13,000 new non-trade-off posts to support the operation of 256 new and upgraded healthcare facilities, with an estimated financial implication of RM1.25 billion.

items.title: Gemas voters get three choices as state election battle begins
items.summary: TAMPIN, July 18 — The Gemas state seat is set for a three-cornered clash in the upcoming 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, with candidates from Parti Wawasan, Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Bersatu all vying for voter attention on Aug 1. Incumbent Gemas assemblyman Ridzuan Ahmad of Parti Wawasan will be defending his seat against Siti Aishah Seman @ Othman (PH) and Azman Abdullah (Bersatu). In the 15th state election, Ridzuan secured 11,653 votes, defeating BN’s Abd.

items.title: Keep Adat Perpatih out of politics, candidates and parties told
items.summary: REMBAU: Parties and candidates contesting in the Negri Sembilan polls should stay away from politicising issues related to the state's unique Adat Perpatih customary system during campaigning. "We do not touch 'adat', we must respect it," said Barisan Nasional deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. Mohamad, who is BN's candidate for Rantau, vowed a healthy campaign in Negri Sembilan.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: Senior citizen withdraws from Sikamat race over incomplete documents
items.summary: Bujang Abu, who had applied to contest the Sikamat seat, was said to have failed to complete the documents required by the Election Commission (EC). The retired civil servant had previously contested the same seat in the 2013 General Election and the 2023 state election, but lost both contests. "I should have submitted the statement to the EC after the election.

items.title: Johor’s new exco line-up set for swearing-in ceremony today
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 18 — The official vehicle carrying the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, arrived at Istana Bukit Serene here at 11.03am today, ahead of the swearing-in ceremony of the Johor State Executive Council (Exco) members for the new term. Earlier, vehicles carrying the new Johor State Exco members began arriving at the palace compound through the Jalan Kolam Air gate at 9am. This was followed by the arrival of vehicles carrying Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, Johor Council of Royal Court president Datuk Dr Abdul Rahim Ramli and State Secretary Datuk Mohammed Ridha Abd Kadir, which entered the palace compound via the main gate at about 10.19am.

items.title: Johari urges BN machinery to run healthy Negri Sembilan polls campaign
items.summary: TAMPIN: Barisan Nasional (BN) election machinery members and supporters have been urged to conduct a healthy campaign throughout the Negri Sembilan election period. Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said this was to ensure the democratic process during the state election campaign period took place in a harmonious atmosphere. "We have two BN candidates in the Tampin parliamentary constituency, namely in the Gemencheh and Repah state seats.

items.title: No quick fix: Expert warns against diabetes jabs for weight loss
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The growing trend of diabetes medication injections for weight-loss without medical supervision carries serious health risks, while depriving diabetic patients of essential medicine, an expert has warned. Universiti Sains Islam Malaysia medical lecturer and consultant physician Dr Mohd Firdaus A. Bakar said the off-label use of diabetes medication solely for weight loss offered no proven clinical benefit for obesity or diabetes. The side effects are real and include nausea, vomiting, drowsiness and gastrointestinal problems," Dr Firdaus said.

items.title: Malaysia's ancient fault lines showing signs of renewed activity, says expert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ancient fault lines in Malaysia are showing signs of more frequent movement and energy release. Among the clearest indicators is the Mersing Fault in Johor, which has been identified as becoming active again after more than a century without any recorded seismic activity in the area. "This shows that fault activity in Peninsular Malaysia is not a new phenomenon.

items.title: Anthony Loke’s Chennah defence begins with straight fight against MCA Youth chief Siow Kong Choon
items.summary: JELEBU, July 18 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke will face a straight fight to defend the Chennah state seat, which he has held since 2013, in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election that begins today. In the 2023 Negeri Sembilan state election, Loke polled 5,888 votes to defeat Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Rosmadi Arif by a 2,200-vote majority. For the latest updates on the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/n9.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: Aminuddin faces three-cornered battle in Linggi
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: The Linggi seat is set for a three-cornered contest in the Negri Sembilan election, with the incumbent, caretaker menteri besar and a former contender for the seat set to face off in a closely watched battle. The Election Commission (EC) confirmed today that incumbent Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli of Barisan Nasional (BN) will take on caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun of Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Datuk Zamri Md Said of Bersatu after nominations closed at 10am. "I received three nomination papers.

items.title: New Felda chairman gets mission to restore and strengthen agency
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — The Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) must continue to be strengthened as an institution tasked with uplifting the dignity of settlers and their families, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Prime Minister said he stressed this during a courtesy call by new Felda chairman Tan Sri Ahmad Badri Mohd Zahir in Putrajaya yesterday. Ahmad Badri was appointed Felda chairman effective July 15 to replace Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek, whose term of service ended on June 30.

items.title: Egypt's 'Garbage City' recyclers reap gains from Iran war plastic squeeze
items.summary: CAIRO, (Egypt): In the labyrinthine alleyways of Cairo's Garbage City, recycling specialist Peter Romany finds himself fielding calls from factories scrambling for plastic to plug supply shortfalls caused by the Iran war. "Before the war, we were the ones calling factories, trying to sell our material," Romany told AFP, standing beside towering bales of compressed plastic. "But after the war broke out, the factories started calling us.

items.title: Penang man’s disappearance mystery ends after family identifies body at morgue
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 18 — A man reported missing after failing to return home on June 10 has been confirmed dead. Barat Daya police chief ACP Anuwal Ab Wahab said Shahir Zainul, 46, was confirmed to have died on June 11, a day after he went missing. His body was identified by family members at the Penang Hospital (HPP) Forensic Department yesterday.

items.title: Dataran Merdeka set for 300,000-strong World Cup final crowd on record-breaking giant screen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Kuala Lumpur is bracing for one of the biggest football watch parties in its history, with police expecting up to 300,000 fans to descend on Dataran Merdeka tomorrow night to watch the Fifa World Cup final. Kuala Lumpur police chief Comm Datuk Fadil Marsus said authorities were expecting a huge turnout as football supporters gather to watch the world’s biggest match under the city skyline. We hope to fill Dataran Merdeka,” he told The Star today.

items.title: Who started the fire? Police investigate burnt classroom furniture in Kampar school
items.summary: IPOH, July 18 — Police have launched an investigation into suspected vandalism at a secondary school in Kampar after several desks and chairs were found burnt in a classroom yesterday. Kampar district police chief Supt Mohamad Nazri Daud said a school prefect discovered the incident at about 10.30am while monitoring classrooms during the break. Mohamad Nazri said the school’s internal investigation could not identify those responsible, leading to a police report being lodged to assist with further investigations.

items.title: KWAP pursues recovery of RM163.4 million eFishery investment after fraud scandal
items.summary: In a statement today, KWAP said its total investment in eFishery amounted to RM163.4 million, representing approximately 2.51 per cent of the company's total shareholding. The Fund also said appropriate follow-up actions have been taken in accordance with KWAP's internal governance and accountability framework. KWAP said the broader eFishery case involved deliberate financial manipulation and misrepresentation of the company's financial reporting.

items.title: Anwar: Felda must continue to be strengthened to uplift settlers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) must continue to be strengthened as an institution tasked with uplifting the dignity of settlers and their families, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Prime Minister said he stressed this during a courtesy call by new Felda chairman Tan Sri Ahmad Badri Mohd Zahir in Putrajaya yesterday. Ahmad Badri was appointed Felda chairman effective July 15 to replace Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek, whose term of service ended on June 30.

items.title: Umno sec-gen says BN-Perikatan understanding puts stability first in Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki today said an understanding between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Negeri Sembilan state election was aimed at ensuring political stability in the state. Speaking after nomination proceedings in Jempol, Asyraf said the priority now was to ensure candidates from the two coalitions won all 36 state seats while keeping the campaign focused on what they could offer voters. Most importantly, we must acknowledge the reality that Negeri Sembilan is a multi-racial and multi-religious state,” he told Berita Harian at the Jempol District Administration Complex after nomination proceedings for the Serting, Palong, Jeram Padang and Bahau state seats.

items.title: Body of man attacked by crocodile found with bite marks
items.summary: KINABATANGAN: The body of a 57-year-old man who was attacked by a crocodile while cleaning a boat at Kampung Batu Putih yesterday has been found. Rusli Mardin was found at 4.45am by villagers, about 500m downstream from where he was last seen. Kinabatangan Fire and Rescue Department chief Suriyadi Darwis said villagers alerted firefighters after discovering the body.

items.title: Tun Faisal faces ex-Melaka deputy police chief in Sikamat three-way fight
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The Sikamat seat will see a three-cornered contest in the Negri Sembilan election. The Sikamat seat was previously held by Negri Sembilan PH chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, who is also the caretaker menteri besar. The Ampangan seat will also see a three-cornered contest involving former Ampangan assemblyman Datuk Dr Rafie Abd Malek, who is contesting as a PN candidate, PH's Datuk Muhammad Nazri Kassim and Noor'azah Harun of Bersatu.

items.title: Jordan's army says shot down 10 Iranian missiles
items.summary: AMMAN, (Jordan): Jordan's army said on Saturday it had shot down 10 Iranian missiles with no casualties or damage, as Teheran retaliated against American strikes by pressing attacks on US allies. "Air defense systems... intercepted 10 Iranian missiles that had entered Jordanian airspace and were targeting the Kingdom's territory (which) were intercepted and shot down," the army said in a statement, adding that there were no casualties or material damage. — AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Hadi says Perikatan and BN will campaign for each other in Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Barisan Nasional (BN) will campaign for each other’s candidates throughout the Negeri Sembilan state election. Abdul Hadi made the remarks after nomination proceedings for the N05 Serting state seat today, Harian Metro reported. Asked whether the cooperation could eventually be formalised, Abdul Hadi said it would be assessed over time.

items.title: No more heart skips: Sarawak achieves first Borneo valve implant breakthrough
items.summary: KUCHING, July 18 — Sarawak Heart Centre has successfully performed Borneo’s first Transcatheter Pulmonary Valve Implantation (TPVI), marking a significant milestone in expanding access to advanced structural heart interventions in East Malaysia. In a Facebook post, Sarawak Heart Centre said the milestone was achieved through close collaboration with an expert team from Hospital Serdang, working alongside its multidisciplinary team. “Sarawak Heart Centre remains committed to expanding access to advanced cardiovascular care, embracing innovation and continuously improving the services we provide to the people of Sarawak and beyond,” it said.

items.title: Sarawak ministry warns public over fake local purchase orders issued under its name
items.summary: KUCHING, July 18 — The Sarawak Ministry of Public Health, Housing and Local Government (MPHLG) has issued a public warning over the circulation of fraudulent local purchase orders (LPOs) falsely bearing the ministry’s name and details. The ministry confirmed that the LPOs currently being circulated are fake, unauthorised and were not issued by MPHLG. It urged suppliers, contractors, service providers and members of the public to verify the authenticity of any LPO purportedly issued by the ministry before accepting orders, providing services or making payments.

items.title: Sarawakian consultant claims AI copied his voice to trick friends into sending money to scammers
items.summary: KUCHING, July 18 — A local feng shui and qimen consultant has claimed that scammers used artificial intelligence (AI) to clone his voice in an attempt to trick his friends into transferring money. Alex Ling said a mutual friend of his and Sarawak United People’s Party (SUPP) Public Complaints Bureau chief Milton Foo received a phone call from someone whose voice closely resembled his. He said the first case involved a hawker at Kenyalang Market earlier this month, where the scammer successfully deceived the victim’s fellow hawkers into transferring money.

items.title: BN big guns rally behind candidates as Negeri Sembilan race begins
items.summary: REMBAU, July 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi accompanied his deputy, Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, to the nomination centre for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election today. All eight nomination centres for the Negeri Sembilan state election opened simultaneously at 9am and closed an hour later. In Port Dickson, BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir accompanied Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli, who is contesting the Linggi state seat, while Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Johari Ghani accompanied Suhaimizan Bizar, who is contesting the Gemencheh state seat in Tampin parliamentary constituency.

items.title: Pakatan’s Negeri Sembilan team enters race with strong show of support
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 18 — The Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidates contesting the Labu, Bukit Kepayang, Rahang, Mambau and Seremban Jaya seats in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election received a boost today when they were accompanied by three deputy ministers to the nomination centre here. The candidates – Nicole Tan Lee Koon (Bukit Kepayang), Siau Meow Kong (Rahang), Ahmad Faez Abdul Razak (Labu), Lee Kai Yet (Mambau) and S. Mugunthan (Seremban Jaya) – marched with the three deputy ministers and thousands of supporters from the Seremban A&W Restaurant to the Seremban City Council Hall at about 8.30am. Tan and Siau are incumbents in their respective constituencies, while Ahmad Faez, Lee and Mugunthan are newcomers fielded by PH to replace the incumbents for the Labu, Mambau and Seremban Jaya state seats, respectively.

items.title: Father’s body found in Sabah river hours after crocodile attack witnessed by teenage son
items.summary: Villagers discovered Rusli’s body and alerted firefighters, who rushed to the scene. Kinabatangan Fire and Rescue Station chief Suriyadi Darwis said the victim’s body was still intact, although there were bite marks on his abdomen and the right side of his neck. “When the victim was found, the victim’s body was still intact, but there were bite marks on his abdomen and the right side of his neck.

items.title: Perak police bust suspected love scam centres in Ipoh, arrest 12 Chinese nationals
items.summary: IPOH, July 18 — Perak police have arrested 12 Chinese nationals in two separate raids in Ipoh over suspected online love scam centres. The latest operation marks the second online scam bust in Ipoh within three days, after police arrested 18 Chinese nationals on July 13 over suspected scam centres allegedly exploiting third-party financial accounts to defraud victims. Based on preliminary investigations and conversation scripts found on laptops seized during the raids, Mohd Alwi said police believe the syndicate was operating online love scam call centres targeting victims in China.

items.title: NUTP backs Teachers’ Protection Act, says lawsuits and online backlash leave educators afraid to discipline students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Teachers are increasingly reluctant to discipline students for fear of being sued, reported to police or vilified on social media, Malaysia’s largest teachers’ union has warned, backing renewed calls for a Teachers’ Protection Act. NUTP secretary-general Fouzi Singon said the pressure on teachers had intensified in recent years, extending beyond the classroom to legal threats, public criticism and online scrutiny. “Teachers today not only face the challenge of educating students, but also have to contend with threats of lawsuits, police reports, social media criticism and pressure from various parties.
